channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish on street and pavements, 11th April
item.description:
Rubbish on street and pavements requires to be taken away and street/pavement/weeds needs to be swept and tidied up.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Borthwick Street, Deptford
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Permanent seating blocking access, 11th April
item.description:
Permanent wooden seating blocking any kind of disabled access to the remaining 2 shops on the parade. Hindering business and making it unsafe for people trying to access those property’s. There are stairs at the beginning if the parade, meaning anyone using a wheelchair or scooter to access those shops need to be able to freely use the pathway outside the shops further up

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 308, Westwood Lane, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass bottles, 11th April
item.description:
Broken glass bottles in the alley way behind the Shell garage, leading from Blackfen Road to Sherwood Park Avenue.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 510, Blackfen Road, Blendon, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking pedestrian access on Vicarage Grove. Usual spots usual stupid users, 11th April
item.description:
This is a daily happening you need to take proper action. Residents are sick and tired of this

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Vicarage Grove, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Holes/trip hazard on narrow footpath, 11th April
item.description:
There are 2 holes in the footpath which appear to be due to tarmac collapsing. Both holes are on a narrow footpath (caused by cars parking officially on the pavement), with the potential of trip hazard, especially at night due to black tarmac. Approximate w3w is ///gift.toward.smart

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lewisham Park, Ladywell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip on pavement, 11th April
item.description:
Cardboard boxes flytipped.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 182, Maple Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Priory park Road near number 38, 11th April
item.description:
Bagged waste left by the tree

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Priory Park Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Not working, 11th April
item.description:
This street light hasn’t worked for awhile even asked numerous reports, with the two lights out in this part of the close it is very dark and very dangerous sometimes because of some of the people that hang out in the area

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Scrutton Close, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped outside shops, 11th April
item.description:
Trip hazard and detrimental to local businesses looks extremely dirty and off putting. Please can it be cleared asap

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Plaistow Lane, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lamp post leaning, 11th April
item.description:
The lamp post is leaning to one side as I’ve had some other neighbours that someone had crashed into it and it is no longer working which makes the area darker and more dangerous

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Scrutton Close, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish not collected, 11th April
item.description:
Please collect and check why bags bot removed after three days. Disgusting refuse

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 226, Upland Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rats from hole in the ground | Been seen on daily basis, 11th April
item.description:
There are rats coming out from a hole in the ground behind the bus stop W on creek road. Hole shown on the picture. They use the litter bin close to the bus stop for food. They can be also seen moving around creek road or the developments behind the bus stop. The hole is on the ground is quite well done and seem to be used a lot meaning they live there and they can be seen on daily basis.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Creek Road, Deptford
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bad algae forming on pond, 11th April
item.description:
Bad green algae developing on pond on Ravensbourne side of the park. Just worried about the baby ducks if it is harmful to them

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravensbourne Greenway, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bright light shining into number 17, 11th April
item.description:
The flood light looks it has been moved again and is shingling directly into our flat.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Braydon Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street sweepers’ sacks dumped by tree., 11th April
item.description:
Young Tree will be damaged by sacks left against its trunk. There is no point in sweeping if you are going to leave sacks. It just creates more littering

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Evering Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 Sacks (originally 4) left by street sweepers for over 3 weeks. Now with faeces adjacent., 11th April
item.description:
The tree outside 29 & 31 Benthal Rd is used by street sweepers as a depot! This is not acceptable. It ENCOURAGES further littering. Pavement at this site is disrupted by tree roots & impossible to clean. Put your sacks on East side of street - more space.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Evering Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish bags dumped again, 11th April
item.description:
Bags of rubbish dumped again. Why aren't the council doing anything to catch the perpetrator? It's happening every other day, the neighbours have to live in filth, its unsightly, causes a hazard as its on the path, gets ravaged by foxes and then blows into the road and up the street. Also the food brings vermin.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Marmadon Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole opposite 245 Kent House Road, 11th April
item.description:
There’s long been a crack in the centre of Kent House Road in the braking zone as vehicle turn right into Thesiger Road. That crack is now opening up to a pothole and is causing large vibrations to surrounding houses as heavy vehicles pass over the pothole and worse still when they brake. This has multiple issues including damaging to houses and risk of tyre failure and loss of grip in a braking zone near a two pedestrian island crossings. The pothole was marked in white chalk some time ago but nothing has been done and the vibrations are getting worse. The road surface is dangerous and not fit for purpose
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kent House Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grove Vale-Overflowing Litterbin in Front of Maxim Chicken Shop, 11th April
item.description:
Litterbin is overflowing with waste and it needs to be emptied/cleared up.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 36, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Jarvis Road-Flytipping, 11th April
item.description:
A trolley has been flytipped and needs to be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Jarvis Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
